metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Woodwork 10 teaches students to design and create functional and beautiful wooden objects using hand tools and machinery. Students learn about wood properties, joinery techniques, finishing, and safety practices. Through projects ranging from small decorative pieces to furniture, they develop precision, problem-solving skills, and an appreciation for the craft while exploring woodworking traditions and sustainable practices.
metadata.general_description: Elective course focused on woodworking techniques, furniture design, and wood craftsmanship.

big_ideas.statement: Wood is a natural material with unique properties that make it ideal for crafting functional and beautiful objects.
big_ideas.elaboration: Different woods have different characteristics. Understanding wood properties—grain, stability, hardness, workability—enables woodworkers to make wise material choices and execute techniques skillfully.

big_ideas.statement: Woodworking combines precision, planning, and problem-solving to create pieces that are both functional and aesthetically pleasing.
big_ideas.elaboration: Success in woodworking requires attention to detail, careful planning, and the ability to troubleshoot when things don't go as expected. Patience and craftsmanship are essential.

big_ideas.statement: Sustainable woodworking respects forests, uses materials responsibly, and honors woodworking traditions across cultures.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students develop awareness of where wood comes from, the environmental impact of harvesting, and how to source materials responsibly. They learn from diverse woodworking traditions.

big_ideas.statement: Design thinking applied to woodworking results in pieces that serve their purpose beautifully and stand the test of time.
big_ideas.elaboration: Through sketching, planning, and iteration, students develop designs that work well and look beautiful. They learn to balance form and function thoughtfully.

competencies.raw: Design functional and beautiful wooden pieces
i_can_statements: I can design furniture and objects that are both functional and attractive
I can sketch designs and create plans
I can adapt designs based on materials and constraints
I can consider user needs in design
I can choose woods appropriate for designs
I can select joinery methods that work for designs
I can plan construction sequences
I can evaluate choices and make refinements

competencies.raw: Explore woodworking traditions and cultural practices
i_can_statements: I can research woodworking traditions from different cultures
I can appreciate historical and contemporary woodwork
I can create inspired pieces respectfully
I can learn from Indigenous woodworking traditions

competencies.raw: Finish and refine work with quality
i_can_statements: I can sand and finish wood pieces professionally
I can stain, oil, or varnish appropriately
I can achieve fine finishes and details
I can evaluate quality and make improvements

competencies.raw: Use hand tools skillfully and safely
i_can_statements: I can use hand tools with precision and control
I can select appropriate tools for tasks
I can work safely with hand tools
I can maintain hand tools properly
I can operate saws, planers, routers, and other equipment safely
I can set up machinery correctly
I can maintain equipment in good condition
I can troubleshoot equipment issues
I can measure and mark accurately
I can cut, shape, and plane wood skillfully
I can execute joinery techniques (mortise, dovetail, etc.)
I can assemble and glue pieces accurately
I can plan and execute complex projects
I can manage time and materials efficiently
I can solve problems that arise
I can maintain a safe and organized workshop
I can explain wood properties and how to select wood
I can understand wood movement and stability
I can identify different wood species
I can apply knowledge to improve work
I can prepare surfaces for finishing
I can apply stains, oils, and finishes
I can troubleshoot finishing problems
I can achieve professional-quality finishes
I can use CAD to design woodworking projects
I can create technical drawings and specifications
I can explore CNC and digital fabrication
I can document and share designs
I can explore specialized joinery and techniques
I can understand advanced finishing methods
I can explore decorative and artistic processes
I can understand industrial woodworking
